description_long: This bungalow features a sprawling, L-shaped layout with a dark, multi-gabled roof and light-colored exterior walls. The structure includes a prominent central section with dormer windows and several wings extending outward, creating a sense of depth and complexity. Large windows are distributed across the facade, with some grouped together and others spaced apart, providing ample natural light. A covered porch is visible, offering a sheltered outdoor area. The building sits low to the ground, typical of bungalow architecture, and the roof overhangs provide additional shading. The overall design suggests a blend of traditional and modern bungalow elements, suitable for a suburban or residential neighborhood.
description: A large, L-shaped bungalow with a dark gabled roof, light exterior walls, and a covered porch. Features include dormer windows, grouped and spaced windows, and a low, sprawling profile.
description_view_0: Front-facing view showing the main entrance, dormer windows, and the covered porch area.
description_view_1: Side elevation highlighting the long wing, multiple windows, and the extended roofline.
description_view_2: Rear perspective displaying the back facade, additional windows, and the continuation of the L-shape.
description_view_3: Opposite side view revealing more windows, the porch extension, and the overall depth of the structure.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical with grouped and spaced windows
